slides.title: One serving of greens, *a sharper brain*.
slides.sub: A daily helping of leafy greens may slow how quickly the brain ages. The habit is small and simple.

slides.title: Greens are brain food
slides.body: People who eat leafy greens daily *tend to stay* sharper as they age. The link shows up again and again.

slides.title: The nutrients involved
slides.body: Folate, lutein and vitamin K fill leafy greens. Together they *may support* memory and clearer thinking.

slides.title: One serving a day
slides.body: A single daily serving is the simple target. Most of the benefit *appears to come* from showing up consistently.

slides.title: Easy ways to eat them
slides.body: Add a handful to a smoothie, soup or omelet, or build a quick side salad. Small servings *add up*.

slides.title: Feed your future mind
slides.body: Make greens a daily habit, not a once-in-a-while side. Your brain *may thank you* for decades.

slides.title: Eat for a *sharper future*.
slides.body: Vibe Health tracks the food, sleep and movement that help you age well, all in one simple daily score.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
A daily serving of leafy greens may slow how quickly the brain ages. 🌿

The folate, lutein and vitamin K inside support memory and clear thinking, and one serving a day is enough to start. Save this. 🔖

Vibe Health turns those food habits into one daily score → link in bio.
youtube_title: How a Daily Serving of Leafy Greens May Slow Brain Aging
